IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We are needing to carry out some maintenance work on a piece of electrical equipment known as a transformer. This is due to homes and businesses adopting low-carbon technologies such as electric vehicles, heat pumps and renewable generation which means the energy demand on the local network is changing. We need to turn off your power so we can carry out a tap change which will change the voltage settings at the transformer to help provide additional capacity for low carbon technologies, improve efficiency and reduce energy losses and help support a cleaner and more sustainable energy network. This will help the needs for net zero now and in the future. This will improve the reliability of our electricity network and ensure it is working to the best of its ability to support net zero. This work should be completed within 15 minutes of the power being turned off, however we like to provide a wider restriction window in case our engineers encounter any issues on the day. This is taking place on Pentstemon Drive.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
Please note that this work was originally scheduled for April but was cancelled due to traffic management issues. A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely.
Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps the network safe and reliable.
Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months.
Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, the power interruption is 5 hours.
This work is taking place near Harrow Road and Old London Road.

We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.

Steps.Message:
Thanks for bearing with us over today.

Our specialist cable jointers have so far been unable to pinpoint the underground cabling fault, so they have instructed our digging gang to excavate our cabling. This will allow them to cut our cable, and using specialist equipment, test each side to help narrow down where the fault lies.

Our estimated time frame doesn't need to change currently, and we'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here on 08003163105 from any phone if you have any questions. Sent by Lisa at 18:21.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 05:40 - Our engineers have restored power to all 2003 properties in the Epping CM16 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the fault on our overhead cabling network.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Bev at 05:09 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground cable fault that initially interrupted power to 2003 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 34 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 06:30 and 07:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Stuart at 08:12 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A fault has occurred on a piece of our electrical equipment which controls the power to your home.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Stuart at 08:12 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the South Street area, the estimated time of 10:30 and 11:30 hasn't changed.

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can.

We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Will at 07:52.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
